do-i-need-a-permit-to-build-a-dock-in-ta.headline: Do I Need a Permit for a Dock in Tampa Bay?
do-i-need-a-permit-to-build-a-dock-in-ta.carouselHook: Every Tampa dock touches 4 agencies. Here's each one.

carousel.title: Do I need a permit?
carousel.body: Almost always yes. Here's what you're up against.

carousel.title: 1. Your county building department
carousel.body: Hillsborough, Pinellas, Manatee, Sarasota, or Pasco. Every dock touches this office.

carousel.title: 2. FDEP
carousel.body: Florida DEP reviews docks over sovereign submerged lands. Most private single-family docks get an exemption but still need to file.

carousel.title: 3. Army Corps of Engineers
carousel.body: Federal review of navigable waters. Nationwide permits are fast. Individual permits can take a year.

carousel.title: 4. Your HOA
carousel.body: Trips up more homeowners than the government does. Check covenants before materials show up on site.

how-much-does-a-dock-cost-in-tampa-2026.headline: How Much a Dock Actually Costs in Tampa
how-much-does-a-dock-cost-in-tampa-2026.carouselHook: The 2026 Tampa dock cost sample. 200 projects, honest numbers.

carousel.title: What a Tampa dock really costs
carousel.body: Median: $15,000. Range: $750 (dinghy dock) to $65,000 (T-shape with lift and roof).

carousel.title: What moves the price
carousel.body: Length. Materials. Water depth. Permit fees. Bottom type. Not the builder's ego.

carousel.title: Pressure-treated pine
carousel.body: $28 per sq ft. Cheapest. Lasts 10 to 15 years in salt. Needs sealing.

carousel.title: Composite decking
carousel.body: $55 per sq ft. Zero maintenance. 25-year warranty. Tampa's default since 2019.

carousel.title: Ipe hardwood
carousel.body: $78 per sq ft. Looks like a yacht deck. Lasts 40 years. Priced accordingly.

floating-vs-fixed-docks-tampa-bay.headline: Floating or Fixed? A Tampa Bay Guide
floating-vs-fixed-docks-tampa-bay.carouselHook: Tampa Bay tides swing 3 ft. Here's how that changes your dock decision.

carousel.title: Floating vs fixed
carousel.body: Tampa's tide isn't dramatic, but it's persistent. That changes the math.

carousel.title: Fixed docks
carousel.body: Rigid platform on pilings. Higher cost. Longer life. Better for open Bay exposure.

carousel.title: Floating docks
carousel.body: Rides the tide. Lower cost. Better boat access at low tide. Higher maintenance in salt.

carousel.title: Where floating wins
carousel.body: Canal-front homes. Kayak launches. Docks that see extreme tidal swing.

carousel.title: Where fixed wins
carousel.body: Open Bay exposure. Larger boats. Homes that will resell to boat owners.

best-dock-materials-saltwater.headline: The Best Dock Materials for Tampa Bay Saltwater
best-dock-materials-saltwater.carouselHook: Four dock materials, ranked for Tampa Bay saltwater. Real lifespans.

carousel.title: What lasts in Tampa salt?
carousel.body: Not everything. Marketing lies. Here's the real ranking.

carousel.title: Pressure-treated pine
carousel.body: 10 to 15 years. Cheapest. Rots. Needs annual sealing. Familiar friend.

carousel.title: Composite
carousel.body: 25 to 30 years. Zero maintenance. Modern Tampa default. Trex, Azek, Fiberon.

carousel.title: Ipe hardwood
carousel.body: 30 to 50 years. Yacht-grade look. Silvers gracefully. Premium price.

carousel.title: Marine aluminum
carousel.body: 40+ years. Won't rot, warp, or splinter. Modern industrial look. Cool underfoot.

dock-seawall-or-bulkhead.headline: Dock, Seawall, or Bulkhead? Which You Actually Need
dock-seawall-or-bulkhead.carouselHook: Dock vs seawall vs bulkhead. Not the same thing. Here's the difference.

carousel.title: Not the same thing
carousel.body: Dock, seawall, and bulkhead get mixed up constantly. They do different jobs.

carousel.title: Dock
carousel.body: Access structure. Extends from the shore out over the water. You walk on it.

carousel.title: Seawall
carousel.body: Vertical retaining wall between land and water. Protects your yard from erosion and surge.

carousel.title: Bulkhead
carousel.body: Basically a seawall. In Florida the terms are used interchangeably.

carousel.title: The order to build them
carousel.body: Seawall first, then dock. Building a dock and then failing to seawall the yard is how projects get expensive fast.

does-adding-a-dock-increase-home-value.headline: Does Adding a Dock Increase Home Value?
does-adding-a-dock-increase-home-value.carouselHook: Dock ROI in Tampa Bay. What realtors actually pay for a new dock.

carousel.title: Does a dock add value?
carousel.body: In Tampa Bay: yes, mostly. But how much depends entirely on where you are.

carousel.title: Deep-water canals
carousel.body: 60% to 90% of dock cost recovered at sale. Sometimes more.

carousel.title: Navigable canals with bridges
carousel.body: 40% to 60% recovery. Every draft-restricted canal ranks lower.

carousel.title: Waterfront without boat access
carousel.body: 20% to 40% recovery. Build for lifestyle, not resale.

carousel.title: The kicker
carousel.body: A failing dock discounts you more than a new one adds. Fix before you list.

12-questions-tampa-dock-builder.headline: 12 Questions Every Tampa Dock Owner Should Ask
12-questions-tampa-dock-builder.carouselHook: 12 questions that separate great Tampa dock builders from the rest.

carousel.title: 12 questions to ask
carousel.body: Before you sign anything, these separate the pros from the storm chasers.

carousel.title: 1. License and insurance
carousel.body: Ask to see the license number. Verify at MyFloridaLicense.com.

carousel.title: 2. Local Bay work
carousel.body: How many Tampa Bay docks in the last 12 months? Get names.

carousel.title: 3. Permits included?
carousel.body: Ask specifically whether county, FDEP, and Army Corps fees are in the quote.

carousel.title: 4. Warranty coverage
carousel.body: Get the warranty in writing. Ask what's excluded.

whats-included-vs-excluded-in-a-dock-quo.headline: What's Actually Included vs Excluded in a Dock Quote
whats-included-vs-excluded-in-a-dock-quo.carouselHook: 12 dock quote line items builders quietly leave out. Know them before signing.

carousel.title: What's really in a quote?
carousel.body: The headline number is not the total. Here's what to double-check.

carousel.title: Survey fees
carousel.body: $800 to $2,500. Rarely included. Ask specifically.

carousel.title: Permit fees
carousel.body: $1,500 to $3,000 across agencies. Some builders quote without.

carousel.title: Electrical
carousel.body: Outlet, lighting, panel run. $2k to $5k. Almost always separate.

carousel.title: Removal of old dock
carousel.body: $40 to $80 per linear foot. Not always priced in.

ready-to-build-get-quotes.headline: You're Ready to Build. Now What?
ready-to-build-get-quotes.carouselHook: Ready to build a Tampa dock? Here's the 5-step plan.

carousel.title: You're ready to build
carousel.body: Great. Here's the 5-step plan to get quotes and pick a builder without getting burned.

carousel.title: Step 1: know your spec
carousel.body: Size, materials, add-ons. Use our cost calculator to lock a range first.

carousel.title: Step 2: get 3 quotes
carousel.body: Not 2. Not 10. Three is the number that surfaces outliers on both ends.

carousel.title: Step 3: verify licenses
carousel.body: MyFloridaLicense.com is free. Skip this and pay 10x later.

carousel.title: Step 4: read the contract
carousel.body: Deposit, milestones, warranty. If it's not in writing, it doesn't exist.
